# Traceability Matrix Schema

YAML schema for `docs/.index/traceability.yaml` rows produced by the `traceability` skill.
This file is the single source of truth for row shape and field semantics.

> **Loading**: Loaded under `tier: standard` and `tier: deep` via the skill's `ref_docs.schema`
> entry. Skip when invoking under `tier: light`.

## Top-Level Document Layout

```yaml
# docs/.index/traceability.yaml
# Generated by /traceability — do not edit by hand
_meta:
  schema: "1.0.0"           # Schema version of this document
  generated: "YYYY-MM-DD"   # ISO 8601 date the matrix was last regenerated
  generator: "traceability" # Skill name that produced this artifact
  source_index_version: ""  # docs/.index/manifest.yaml _meta.schema, copied verbatim
  rows: N                   # Total matrix rows (one per requirement_id)
  findings: N               # Validation finding count from the last run

rows:
  - <row>                   # See "Row Schema" below
  - <row>
  - ...
```

`_meta.schema` follows semantic versioning. Consumers must check `_meta.schema` before
parsing rows; mismatched majors mean the schema has changed in a backward-incompatible way.

## Row Schema

Every row is one requirement and the evidence chain that satisfies it.

```yaml
- requirement_id: SRS-CALC-001          # MANDATORY — primary key
  design_id: SI-CALC                    # OPTIONAL — design item that allocates this requirement
  code_paths:                           # MANDATORY (may be empty list) — implementation files
    - src/calc/engine.cpp
    - src/calc/engine.hpp
  test_ids:                             # MANDATORY (may be empty list) — test cases that verify this requirement
    - TC-CALC-001
    - TC-CALC-002
  risk_ids:                             # MANDATORY (may be empty list) — hazards mitigated by this requirement
    - H-03
  clause_refs:                          # MANDATORY (may be empty list) — standard-clause references
    - IEC-62304-5.1.1
    - ISO-13485-7.3.3
  soup_ids: []                          # OPTIONAL (may be empty list) — SOUP record IDs this requirement depends on; populated from docs/.index/soup.yaml when present
  status: complete                      # MANDATORY — see "Status Field" below
  notes: ""                             # OPTIONAL — free-form notes (kept for human review)
```

### Field Definitions

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `requirement_id` | string | Yes | Format `SRS-{CAT}-{NNN}`. Primary key for the row. Must resolve via `router.yaml` `id_routes.SRS`. |
| `design_id` | string | No | Format `SI-{CODE}`. Omit if the requirement has no design allocation yet. |
| `code_paths` | list[string] | Yes (may be empty) | Repository-relative file paths. Empty list is allowed; signals `incomplete` status. |
| `test_ids` | list[string] | Yes (may be empty) | Format `TC-{CAT}-{NNN}`. Empty list signals a `requirement_without_test` finding. |
| `risk_ids` | list[string] | Yes (may be empty) | Format `H-{NN}`. Empty list is allowed and does not flag a finding by default. |
| `clause_refs` | list[string] | Yes (may be empty) | Format `<STANDARD>-<NUMBER>`, e.g. `IEC-62304-5.1.1`. Validated against `compliance/` when that directory exists. |
| `soup_ids` | list[string] | No (may be empty) | Format `SOUP-{NNN}`. SOUP records this requirement depends on; populated from `docs/.index/soup.yaml` when present (produced by the `soup-inventory` skill). Empty list and omission are both permitted. |
| `status` | enum | Yes | One of `complete`, `incomplete`, `deferred`, `needs_review`. See table below. |
| `notes` | string | No | Free-form, single line. Auditors read this; keep it factual. |

### Status Field

The `status` value is derived from the row's cell completeness, not asserted by hand.

| Status | Derivation rule |
|--------|-----------------|
| `complete` | All of `code_paths`, `test_ids`, `risk_ids` (when the requirement is safety-relevant), and `clause_refs` (when `compliance/` exists) are non-empty. |
| `incomplete` | At least one of the cells above is empty without an explicit deferral. |
| `deferred` | Row carries an explicit `notes:` containing `deferred:` followed by a justification. Findings on this row are downgraded from error to warning. |
| `needs_review` | Schema mismatch detected (e.g. a `requirement_id` exists in the catalogue but the row was not regenerated). Triggers a `dangling_*` finding. |

The skill writes one of the four values; consumers must not invent new ones.

## Identifier Format Conventions

Identifiers in matrix rows must match the formats produced by `doc-index`. These match the
ID patterns documented in `streamliner-docs-hub.md` and consumed by `router.yaml`
`id_routes`:

| Entity | Format | Example | Source `id_route` |
|--------|--------|---------|-------------------|
| Requirement | `SRS-{CAT}-{NNN}` | `SRS-CALC-001` | `id_routes.SRS` |
| Design item | `SI-{CODE}` | `SI-CALC`, `SI-DG` | `id_routes.SI` |
| Interface | `IF-{NNN}` | `IF-001` | `id_routes.IF` |
| Test case | `TC-{CAT}-{NNN}` | `TC-CALC-001` | `id_routes.TC` |
| Hazard | `H-{NN}` | `H-03` | `id_routes.H` |
| Use scenario | `HUS-{NN}` | `HUS-01` | `id_routes.HUS` |
| Screen | `SCR-{NNN}` | `SCR-001` | `id_routes.SCR` |
| Flow | `FLW-{NNN}` | `FLW-001` | `id_routes.FLW` |

### Clause Reference Format

`clause_refs` entries have the form `<STANDARD>-<NUMBER>` where `<STANDARD>` is uppercase
with hyphens preserved (`IEC-62304`, `ISO-13485`, `ISO-14971`, `ISO-26262`, `IEC-61508`)
and `<NUMBER>` is the dotted clause path verbatim from the standard.

Examples:

| String | Standard | Clause |
|--------|----------|--------|
| `IEC-62304-5.1.1` | IEC 62304 | 5.1.1 |
| `ISO-13485-7.3.3` | ISO 13485 | 7.3.3 |
| `ISO-14971-5.4` | ISO 14971 | 5.4 |
| `ISO-26262-6-7.4.13` | ISO 26262 part 6 | 7.4.13 |

The skill never invents clause references — it only records ones already cited in the
project's `compliance/` rule files or in document frontmatter.

## Example: Minimal Matrix

A small but complete `traceability.yaml` for a project with one requirement:

```yaml
_meta:
  schema: "1.0.0"
  generated: "2026-05-04"
  generator: "traceability"
  source_index_version: "1.0.0"
  rows: 1
  findings: 0

rows:
  - requirement_id: SRS-CALC-001
    design_id: SI-CALC
    code_paths:
      - src/calc/engine.cpp
      - src/calc/engine.hpp
    test_ids:
      - TC-CALC-001
    risk_ids:
      - H-03
    clause_refs:
      - IEC-62304-5.1.1
    status: complete
    notes: ""
```

## Example: Row With Deferral

```yaml
- requirement_id: SRS-PERF-007
  design_id: SI-RG
  code_paths: []
  test_ids: []
  risk_ids: []
  clause_refs:
    - IEC-62304-5.5.2
  status: deferred
  notes: "deferred: blocked by upstream dependency #602; revisit at v1.2 milestone"
```

The `notes:` `deferred:` prefix is the contract that downgrades validation findings on this
row from error to warning.

## Schema Evolution

Schema changes are versioned via `_meta.schema`. The current major is `1`. Bump rules:

| Change | Bump |
|--------|------|
| Add a new optional field | Minor — `1.0.0` → `1.1.0`. Older consumers ignore the field. |
| Rename or remove a field | Major — `1.x.x` → `2.0.0`. Consumers must guard on the major. |
| Change the meaning of an existing field | Major. |
| Adjust status derivation rules | Minor (consumers re-read this file). |

The `traceability` skill writes the matching schema version into `_meta.schema` on every
regeneration. `--check-only` mode reads `_meta.schema` first and refuses to validate when
the major does not match the schema this file documents.
